process_details: Physical mixtures prepared by gentle mortar mixing for 5 min, dried 10 min at 373 K (to remove water from IDP hemihydrate), then melted in aluminum dish on hot plate at T = 440 K for <=2 min. Vitrification by fast transfer of dish from hot plate to a cold copper plate. Samples analyzed immediately after preparation.
detection_methods: XRD (Rigaku-Denki D/MAX RAPID II-R, Ag K_alpha); DSC (Mettler-Toledo DSC 1, 5 and 10 K/min); TMDSC (TOPEM, 0.5 K/min); BDS (Novo-Control Alpha); ATR-FTIR.
source_quote: Even a small amount of IDP drug (8.8 wt %) is able to suppress the EZB's recrystallization for at least 72 days, when the mixture is stored at 297 K. ... after 72 days, all investigated EZB-IDP mixtures were still physically stable, i.e., we did not observe any Bragg peaks in XRD patterns.
notes: Storage T = 297 K (= 23.85 C), RH = 25% (quasi-constant). DSC at 5 K/min showed no recrystallization for any mixture. Tg from DSC 10 K/min (Table 1, second column). Original paper also reports TMDSC 0.5 K/min values which differ by 1-3 K. Pure EZB recrystallized within 21 days (Dc=15%) under same conditions, providing strong reference for stabilization effect. Authors found no specific molecular EZB-IDP interactions (FTIR); stabilization attributed to antiplasticizing effect of higher-Tg IDP.
review_reasons: Protokolla 297 K (24 C) / 25% RH ei sovi mihinkaan standardiluokkaan: ei ICH Q1A long-term (60% RH), ei dry_short_term (RH ei 0% eika <30 vrk), ei accelerated

process_details: Physical mixtures prepared by gentle mortar mixing for 5 min, dried 10 min at 373 K, then melted in aluminum dish on hot plate at T = 440 K for <=2 min. Vitrification by fast transfer to cold copper plate.
detection_methods: XRD; DSC; TMDSC (0.5 K/min); BDS; ATR-FTIR.
source_quote: after 72 days, all investigated EZB-IDP mixtures were still physically stable, i.e., we did not observe any Bragg peaks in XRD patterns.
notes: Tg from DSC 10 K/min (Table 1, second column). Original paper also reports TMDSC 0.5 K/min values which differ by 1-3 K. Sivulla 5 mainitaan 14.9 wt% 5:1-suhteelle, mikä eroaa Table 1:n arvosta 19.9 wt%. Massoista laskettu 1*365.83/(5*409.43+365.83) = 15.2%, joten 14.9% on lähempänä laskennallista arvoa - Table 1:ssä ilmeisesti painovirhe.
review_reasons: Protokolla 297 K / 25% RH ei sovi standardiluokkaan

Lähteen sisäinen ristiriita: Table 1 antaa 5:1-suhteen wt%(IDP)=19.9, mutta sivulla 5 sama suhde ilmoitetaan arvolla 14.9 wt%. Massoista laskettu arvo (~15.2%) tukee 14.9-arvoa; Table 1 sisältää ilmeisesti painovirheen.

process_details: Physical mixtures prepared by gentle mortar mixing for 5 min, dried 10 min at 373 K, then melted in aluminum dish on hot plate at T = 440 K for <=2 min. Vitrification by fast transfer to cold copper plate.
detection_methods: XRD; DSC; TMDSC (0.5 K/min); BDS; ATR-FTIR.
source_quote: after 72 days, all investigated EZB-IDP mixtures were still physically stable, i.e., we did not observe any Bragg peaks in XRD patterns.
notes: Tg from DSC 10 K/min (Table 1, second column). Original paper also reports TMDSC 0.5 K/min values which differ by 1-3 K. Isothermal DSC at 398 K (Fig 7) confirmed slowed crystallization with IDP content vs. pure EZB.

process_details: Physical mixtures prepared by gentle mortar mixing for 5 min, dried 10 min at 373 K, then melted in aluminum dish on hot plate at T = 440 K for <=2 min. Vitrification by fast transfer to cold copper plate.
detection_methods: XRD; DSC; TMDSC (0.5 K/min); BDS; ATR-FTIR.
source_quote: X-ray diffraction patterns for ... EZB 1:1 IDP measured after specified time period ... after 72 days, all investigated EZB-IDP mixtures were still physically stable, i.e., we did not observe any Bragg peaks in XRD patterns.
notes: Tg from DSC 10 K/min (Table 1, second column). Original paper also reports TMDSC 0.5 K/min values which differ by 1-3 K. XRD time series shown explicitly in Figure 8c (amorphous halos at 14, 21, 29, 39 and 72 days).
review_reasons: Protokolla 297 K / 25% RH ei sovi standardiluokkaan

process_details: Physical mixtures prepared by gentle mortar mixing for 5 min, dried 10 min at 373 K, then melted in aluminum dish on hot plate at T = 440 K for <=2 min. Vitrification by fast transfer to cold copper plate.
detection_methods: XRD; DSC; TMDSC (0.5 K/min); BDS; ATR-FTIR.
source_quote: after 72 days, all investigated EZB-IDP mixtures were still physically stable, i.e., we did not observe any Bragg peaks in XRD patterns.
notes: Tg from DSC 10 K/min (Table 1, second column). Original paper also reports TMDSC 0.5 K/min values which differ by 1-3 K. Mixture has highest IDP content; lowest fragility parameter mp = 78 (vs 93 pure EZB) suggesting strongest stabilization.
review_reasons: Protokolla 297 K / 25% RH ei sovi standardiluokkaan
